disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Abrupt severe chest pain that reached maximum intensity within seconds of onset while lifting a heavy object
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me exactly what happened when the pain started?
Did the pain come on gradually or all at once?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on out of nowhere while I was lifting a box — it went from nothing to the worst pain of my life in a couple of seconds.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain has a tearing/ripping quality and radiates upward into the neck and jaw
trigger_question_examples: How would you describe the pain?
Does the pain move or spread anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a tearing, ripping feeling deep inside, and it shot straight up into my neck and jaw.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Transient loss of consciousness immediately after the pain began
trigger_question_examples: Did you faint or black out at any point?
Did you lose consciousness when this started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — right after the pain hit I blacked out for a few seconds before I came round.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Ongoing weakness, lightheadedness and breathlessness since regaining consciousness
trigger_question_examples: How do you feel right now?
Are you feeling faint, weak or short of breath?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Since I came round I feel really weak and lightheaded, and I'm short of breath.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home blood-pressure readings differ markedly between the two arms (right much higher than left)
trigger_question_examples: Have you managed to check your blood pressure?
If you have a home cuff, can you measure it on both arms for me?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I checked with my home cuff — my right arm read 185 over 100 but my left arm was only about 145 over 85.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long history of poorly controlled hypertension with irregular use of the prescribed blood-pressure tablet
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions?
Do you take any regular medicines, and do you take them as prescribed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had high blood pressure for years and I know it's not well controlled — I'm supposed to take amlodipine but I don't take it reliably.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profuse sweating, pallor and a sense that something is terribly wrong
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself?
Are you sweating or feeling clammy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm pouring with sweat and pale, and I've got this awful feeling that something is really wrong.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No preceding forceful vomiting or retching before the pain began
trigger_question_examples: Were you vomiting or retching before the pain started?
Did the pain follow any vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I wasn't being sick at all — the pain just came on suddenly while I was lifting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is not pleuritic and not clearly positional; it is not worsened specifically by breathing
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain get worse when you breathe in?
Does changing position change the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's not really about breathing — it's a deep tearing pain that's there all the time no matter what I do.
